headline: Is It Time To Reassess Workday (WDAY) After Its One-Year Share Price Slump?
summary: If you are wondering whether Workday shares still reflect the underlying business or if the current price is indicating a possible opportunity, focusing on valuation can help you frame that question more clearly. The stock closed at US$147.18 recently, with a 5.8% gain over the last 7 days, set against declines of 8.7% over 30 days, 28.5% year to date and 42.0% over 1 year, and 18.9% over 3 years and 42.8% over 5 years. These mixed returns have unfolded as investors have been reacting to...

headline: Workday, Inc. (WDAY) Price Target Lowered to $148 at Citi
summary: Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ:WDAY) is among the 10 Stocks That Could Skyrocket in 2026. On February 26, Citi lowered the firm’s price target on Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ:WDAY) to $148 from $247 while maintaining a Neutral rating. The day prior, Morgan Stanley reduced its price target to $185 from $200 and reiterated an Equal Weight rating, noting […]

headline: Hundreds of applications, no jobs and AI replacements: California's brutal tech work landscape
summary: Thousands of tech workers have already lost their jobs this year and leaders at some companies say AI will enable employers to do more with smaller teams.

headline: Workday Founder Returns As CEO While AI Focus Shapes Valuation Outlook
summary: Workday co founder Aneel Bhusri is returning as CEO following the resignation of Carl Eschenbach. The leadership change comes as Workday increases its focus on artificial intelligence across its enterprise software products. The company is responding to sector wide questions about AI, including regulatory attention on potential bias in hiring algorithms. For investors watching NasdaqGS:WDAY, this leadership shift arrives after a mixed share price experience. The stock trades at $147.18,...
